article_title: OpenAI Replaces Its 2018 Charter Tone With Five Looser Principles, Hours Before the Musk Trial Opens
summary: Substantively excellent OpenAI principles + Musk trial framing. All five principles + key quotes verbatim from OpenAI page; Euronews + Al Jazeera claims mostly verify. One unsourced claim: trial duration (~4 weeks) and Satya Nadella testimony are NOT in cited Al Jazeera snapshot.

findings.message: Unsourced: 'trial, which is expected to last about four weeks and feature testimony from Musk, Altman, and Microsoft chief executive Satya Nadella'
findings.details: Al Jazeera (source-2) is the only cited source on the trial and does not mention either the ~4-week duration or Satya Nadella as a witness. Source from a different outlet (Reuters, Bloomberg, Court documents, Forbes) or remove. The Musk/Altman testimony framing is supported (they are named parties) but the trial-duration estimate and Nadella testimony specifically need a citation.

content_preview.title: OpenAI Replaces Its 2018 Charter Tone With Five Looser Principles, Hours Before the Musk Trial Opens
content_preview.summary: Sam Altman published a five-principle framework for OpenAI on April 26, dropping the 2018 charter's pledge to step aside for a safer competitor and reframing the company as AI infrastructure for humanity, just before jury selection in Elon Musk's $134 billion lawsuit.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

OpenAI chief executive Sam Altman published a new five-principle framework for the company on Sunday, April 26, 2026, the most prominent restatement of its mission since the 2018 Charter. The document, titled simply ["Our principles"](https://openai.com/index/our-principles/), organizes the lab's stated commitments around democratization, empowerment, universal prosperity, resilience, and adaptability. It arrived roughly twelve hours before jury selection began in Elon Musk's lawsui...

recommendations: Source or remove the trial-duration / Satya Nadella testimony claim.

editor_notes.content_quality: Strong AI/Policy News piece (~763 words, in News range). Excellent timing framing (principles published 12h before trial), structurally clean.
editor_notes.source_verification: Read all 3 local snapshots. OpenAI page (source-0) verifies all 5 principle names + 4 verbatim quotes (resist consolidation, democratic processes, empower everyone, transparent about when/how/why). Universal prosperity rationale (compute / vertical integration / global data centers) verbatim. Resilience biosecurity + cyber framing verbatim. Euronews verifies AGI deprioritization, merge-and-assist removal (paraphrased), 'held in a decentralised way by people' verbatim, 'much larger force' verbatim, government-cooperation framing. Al Jazeera verifies Oakland, Judge Gonzalez Rogers, 'in the dark' quote, 'wealth machine' quote, 2015 nonprofit founding primarily Musk-funded, $852bn valuation. The April 28 jury-selection date is consistent (Al Jazeera published April 27 saying 'Monday'; April 28 = Monday).
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All material claims verify EXCEPT the trial duration (~4 weeks) and Satya Nadella testimony list, which are not in the cited Al Jazeera source. Single fix.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral, analytical. Hedged on what the new principles actually mean in practice. Internal cross-link to OpenAI $122B / $852bn funding article valid.
editor_notes.originality: New topic, distinct from yesterday's AI/ML coverage.
concerns: Trial-duration + Satya Nadella testimony unsourced from cited Al Jazeera piece.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Excellent timely framing of OpenAI's principle change against the Musk trial backdrop. One specific source/remove fix and ready for re-review.
